title: phlox austromontana subsp. lutescens
intro: Phlox austromontana subsp. lutescens, also known as Yellow Mountain Phlox, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the phlox family and is native to the mountains of New Mexico and Colorado in the United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Phlox austromontana subsp. lutescens include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into salves, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
